title: rootful container privileges from nested child process Linux
body:
### Issue Description

In side the container, we want to fuse mount cvmfs but the it failed whenever running in a nested child process.
I trace the process within container I found out that it started with uid=107 gid=115 but then switch to uid=0 gid=0 later on. I suspect that normal user does not have the write permission and switch to root. 
The container works alright if executed from parent.
How should I change the command to gain root 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a simple electron app that exec podman run container
2. Use app.relaunch() to restart app and run container again
3. `podman pull vnmd/neurodesktop:2024-01-12 && podman run -d --shm-size=1gb -it --privileged --user=root --name neurodeskapp -p 45447:45447 -v neurodesk-home:/home/jovyan`


### Describe the results you received

cvmfs mount with root and normal user
```bash
clone res=0 exe=/usr/bin/cvmfs2 args=-o.rw,system_mount,fsname=cvmfs2,allow_other,grab_mountpoint,uid=107,gid=115.neurodesk.ardc.edu.au./cvmfs/neurodesk.ardc.edu.au. 
tid=82479(cvmfs2) pid=82479(cvmfs2) ptid=82478(cvmfs2) cwd=<NA> fdlimit=131072 pgft_maj=0 pgft_min=1 
vm_size=30604 vm_rss=6100 vm_swap=0 comm=cvmfs2 
cgroups=cpuset=/user.slice.cpu=/user.slice.cpuacct=/.io=/user.slice.memory=/user.slice/user-1002.slice/user@1002.service/user.slice/libpod-1c77c136bcd898335d815b6ddf1a421d4452baf12d5b4b1a85c62cab01e6c38d.scope/container.devices=/.freezer=/.net_cls=/.perf_event=/user.slice/user-1002.slice/user@1002.service/user.slice/libpod-1c77c136bcd898335d815b6ddf1a421d4452baf12d5b4b1a85c62cab01e6c38d.scope/container.net_prio=/.hugetlb=/.pids=/user.slice/user-1002.slice/user@1002.service/user.slice/libpod-1c77c136bcd898335d815b6ddf1a421d4452baf12d5b4b1a85c62cab01e6c38d.scope/container.rdma=/.misc=/. 
flags=562036736(CLONE_CHILD_CLEARTID|CLONE_CHILD_SETTID) 
uid=107 gid=115 vtid=98 vpid=98 pidns_init_start_ts=385905454630315

clone res=99 exe=/usr/bin/cvmfs2 args=__cachemgr__...9.10.5242880000.2621440000.0.3.-1.:. 
tid=82479(cvmfs2) pid=82479(cvmfs2) ptid=82338(tini) cwd=<NA> fdlimit=131072 
pgft_maj=0 pgft_min=385 vm_size=16688 vm_rss=6828 vm_swap=0 comm=cvmfs2 
cgroups=cpuset=/user.slice.cpu=/user.slice.cpuacct=/.io=/user.slice.memory=/user.slice/user-1002.slice/user@1002.service/user.slice/libpod-1c77c136bcd898335d815b6ddf1a421d4452baf12d5b4b1a85c62cab01e6c38d.scope/container.devices=/.freezer=/.net_cls=/.perf_event=/user.slice/user-1002.slice/user@1002.service/user.slice/libpod-1c77c136bcd898335d815b6ddf1a421d4452baf12d5b4b1a85c62cab01e6c38d.scope/container.net_prio=/.hugetlb=/.pids=/user.slice/user-1002.slice/user@1002.service/user.slice/libpod-1c77c136bcd898335d815b6ddf1a421d4452baf12d5b4b1a85c62cab01e6c38d.scope/container.rdma=/.misc=/. 
flags=562036736(CLONE_CHILD_CLEARTID|CLONE_CHILD_SETTID) 
uid=0 gid=0 vtid=98 vpid=98 pidns_init_start_ts=0
```

cvmfs mount with only normal user

### Privileged Or Rootless

I appreciate any insights on this bug. Thanks heaps!!
